# terrible_ravendb_ravendb_index (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `index_name` (String) Name of the index to create, delete, or modify. Must consist only of letters, numbers, dashes, and underscores.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `cluster_wide` (Boolean) Whether to apply enable/disable operations cluster-wide.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `index_configuration` (String) Per-index configuration key/value pairs to reconcile. Keys and values are normalized to strings and compared against the current index definition's configuration. If differences exist, the module updates the definition with the merged configuration.
- `index_definition` (String) Dictionary defining the index (`map` list and optional `reduce` string). Required when creating a new index. When present for an existing index, differences are applied idempotently.
- `mode` (String) Operational mode to apply to an existing index (one of enable/disable/pause/resume/reset). If the index does not exist and only `mode` is provided, the task fails with guidance to create it first.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) Desired state of the index. If `present`, the index will be created if it does not exist, and the definition/configuration will be reconciled. If `absent`, the index will be deleted if it exists. If omitted (`null`), the module operates in "reconcile" mode on existing indexes only (definition, configuration, and/or `mode`). If the index does not exist and only `mode` is provided, the task fails with guidance to use `state=present`.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `msg` (String) Human-readable message describing the result or error.
